starting_conditions_en: Irish culture Capital is in Ireland region
how_to_en: Play the Rags to Riches scenario as Petty King Murchad of Munster. Fabricating claims is sufficient for this, as most of Ireland is divided among independent counts. name_en: The Things Love Does for Us
description_en: Have a Lover save you from a Murder attempt
requirements_en: A lover saved the player from a murder attempt.
how_to_en: In 1066 play as Count Otger of Spreewald. He starts with a rival who has high Intrigue and a wife who is his soulmate. Wait for your rival to attempt to murder you and restart until your soulmate saves you. The Mortal Adoration perk is not necessary but greatly increases the odds. Do not give your lover titles as this will prevent it from triggering. Since 1.13 and the scheme rework, it might be easier achieve it playing as Jarl Haesteinn, the Count of Montigue in 867, become your wife's soulmate, fire your spymaster and wait for the murder attempt by Charles the Bald of France since he's the most well-connected rival.

description_en: As a French Catholic, hold and Completely Control the Kingdom of Thessalonika without holding or being vassalized to the Byzantine Empire
requirements_en: Completely controls the Kingdom of Thessalonika French culture Catholic faith Byzantine Empire Roman Empire Liege owns the Byzantine Empire Liege owns the Roman Empire
how_to_en: Note that you don't need to hold the Kingdom of Thessalonika title, only all its counties. Replace the Byzantine emperor from 1066 with a custom character of French culture and Catholic faith and traits that will offset the Opinion loss. Find a way to conquer enough territory from Hungary and Pechenegs to create the Empire of Carpathia. Then destroy the Byzantine Empire title. This achievement is possible to do in under 10 years as Haesteinn. With the Bellem Justum perk conquering an easy duchy like Brittany or Cornwall (and forming the title) and doing a pilgrimage will give you the necessary prestige and piety to use the Invade Kingdom casus belli against the Byzantines. Focus on Bondí to counter the Byzantines Kataphraktoí. Alternatively, after completing Miklagarðaríki (if you have the Northern Lords DLC), move your capital to France and convert to the local faith and culture to get the achievement instantly.

description_en: Starting as Rurik the Troublemaker in 867, lead your Dynasty to rule the Empire of Russia
requirements_en: Empire of Russia
starting_conditions_en: Starting as Duke Rurik Rurikid of Novgorod in 867
how_to_en: The Kingdom of Novgorod can be created right from the start. Then, to form the Empire of Russia within Rurik's lifetime, you need to do as the Varangians did and convert to the local culture and religion. Your neighbors will be happy and accept vassalage if you are de jure their liege (some gifts might be required). Use your once-per-lifetime Subjugation casus belli to conquer Minsk, then continue to use the Conquer Duchy casus belli. Alternatively, you can start multiple murder plots to stress suicide Rurik, allowing you to play as his only heir, a 17-year-old who also uses the martial tree. His stats are worse than Rurik's, but you have much longer to form Russia before succession issues.

requirements_en: Is Hindu Owns or is liege of the owner of the Counties of: Varanasi Ayodhyā Mathura Sthanisvara Kanchipuram Ujjayini Dvaraka
how_to_en: Several Hindu faiths have holy sites that are unrelated to this achievement. Smartism or Kalikula Shaktism have the correct holy sites for this achievement. You can still get the achievement as any Hindu faith, just use the Smartism religion menu to find the necessary counties. In 867 Maharaja Bhoja of Pratihara (Khosala) starts in control of all but one of the Smartism Holy Sites. If you convert to Smartism or Kalikula Shaktism you only need to gain a casus belli against a Duke ruler. Holders of the sites do not actually have to be of your religion, just need to hold the counties or be the liege of the holders.

description_en: While holding any Empire Title, be naked. May not be available for characters created in the Ruler Designer.
requirements_en: Empire title Is naked
starting_conditions_en: Owns an Empire title and is naked
how_to_en: Starting in 1066, replace the King of France with a custom character of the Papuan or Nakavaram Culture and the traits Temperate, Forgiving, Compassionate, and Honest. The character should be at least 42 with the Fortune Builder education. Aim to have as high Stewardship as possible ( Diplomacy is also important to deal with the negative opinion your vassals will have from cultural acceptance, but other skills are completely unnecessary). Choose the Wealth Focus Lifestyle, and obtain all of the traits of the Avaricious tree, then immediately take the decisions Sell Trivial Titles and Extort Subjects (pick the option that gives the highest gold). Before unpausing, find a spouse with the highest Stewardship (they will be lowborn, but that is fine) and set them to manage domain. Set your Lodging Standards to the highest and every other Court Amenity to the lowest. Invite the character Konan mab Konan de Rennes to your court and once he agrees, declare war on the Duchy of Britanny for his claim. Seiging two castles and winning one battle should be enough to win the war, which should take less than 6 months. Once you have 1250 gold (you should already be very close), create the Kingdom of Britanny and then the Empire of Francia.

description_en: Have Strong Hooks on three Direct Vassals
requirements_en: 3 Strong Hooks on vassals
how_to_en: Using the predominant Homosexuality game rule, choose a ruler under a faith which criminalizes it—Björn Ironside in 867 is a safe option—then wait a few years for vassals to start their criminal love affairs for you to find. Also, with the Truth is Relative perk, your spymaster can fabricate Strong Hooks with the Find Secrets task. The task typically fabricates Hooks on courtiers, not rulers, but you can use it to get Hooks on your own courtiers and then land them. You can start in 867 as any of the Igbo culture Chiefs and simply conquer enough to become a High Chief (Ajapado). You can use the raiding intent of capture. Make use of the Igbo culture tradition of Recognition of Talent and negotiate release of prisoners by recruiting them, this will provide each new courtier with a strong hook. Once you have enough land to hand out in your duchy, provide each of your 3 courtiers with strong hooks with a Chiefdom.

how_to_en: Sometimes the character starts with the Meritocracy perk, allowing the Claim Throne scheme to be used from the start with adequate success chance. Alternatively, try allying France on day 1 and immediately declaring an independence war on your liege, then using your French ally for the two back-to-back wars you'll need to take the south. Divorcing Duke Nuño’s first wife can help secure a useful alliance. You can also align with William the Conqueror early, but his special troops will be preoccupied with England for a while. With the Fate of Iberia DLC you can Found Portugal without being independent as long as it's the Opportunity Phase (which the game begins in).

description_en: Starting as an Iberian Christian, convert all of Iberia to Christianity
requirements_en: Completely controls the Iberia region All counties in the Iberia region is Christian Iberian heritage Christian religion
starting_conditions_en: Iberian heritage Christian religion
how_to_en: In 867 replace the Umayyad Sultan with a custom character of Andalusian culture, Apostolic faith, learning education and pick traits that will offset the Opinion loss (Gregarious is a great option for all Muslims, while Just is a virtuous trait to Muslims, making them like you more. Temperate is also good, as it is considered a virtue by Muslims and any Christian faith with the Monasticism tenet, including Catholicism). Surrender in the starting wars. Focus on the Theologian lifestyle tree and conquer the independent counties before Asturias gets the chance. Then get the three island counties to the east, followed by Navarra and finally Asturias. If the Fate of Iberia DLC is installed keep the Iberian Struggle in the Opportunity phase as long as possible. Alternatively, if the Fate of Iberia DLC is enabled, you can follow the same instructions as above, but starting with a Mozarab ruler instead, as you will automatically be involved with the Struggle and can use the Struggle Casus Bellis. This will also help you unlock the Mozarabic Inquisition achievement.

description_en: Starting as any of the Jimena siblings in 1066, become Emperor of Spain
requirements_en: Empire Empire of Hispania
starting_conditions_en: In 1066, is Starting as either: King Alfonso VI of León King García of Galicia King Sancho II of Castille Countess Urraca of Zamora Elvira
how_to_en: If the Fate of Iberia DLC is installed disable it so you don't have to end the Iberian Struggle in order to create the Empire of Hispania title. Start as Alfonso, he is a high Intrigue character—with some luck and preparation you can kill your two brothers within two years to get their kingdoms (you might need to wait a few months after starting the game before agents are willing to join your murder plots). Then, press your claim on the Kingdom of Navarra. At this point, you can use the Unite the Spanish Thrones decision to unite León, Galicia, Castille and Navarra, giving you a strong core from which you can expand into the rest of the Iberian peninsula until you can create the Empire of Hispania. It is not necessary to complete this achievement in one character's lifetime. Alternatively, consider leaving the DLC enabled. Once you are in position to end the Struggle, do so (do not take the Status Quo ending as it destroys the Hispania title), then create the empire. Convert to Mozarabism and begin converting counties. You can get a plethora of achievements this way. It is possible to get upwards of ten different achievements in runs that begin in Iberia; consider combining this one with one or more of those, such as Rise from the Ashes, Iberia or Iberia?, Darius Revenge, and more. In my run I was able to get fifteen achievements ranging from Struggle endings to surviving the Black Death, all the way until End of an Era in 1453.

description_en: Starting as Robert the Fox in 1066, rule the Kingdom of Sicily, hold either the Kingdom of Epirus, Hellas, or Thessalonika, and have converted all of its original Counties to Catholicism
requirements_en: One of the following: Owns the Kingdom of Sicily Owns the Kingdom of Naples Owns the Kingdom of Trinacria One of the following: Completely Controls the Kingdom of Epirus All Counties are Catholic Completely Controls the Kingdom of Thessalonika All Counties are Catholic Completely Controls the Kingdom of Hellas All Counties are Catholic
starting_conditions_en: Starting as Duke Robert de Hauteville of Apulia in 1066
how_to_en: Shortly after the game begins an event will trigger allowing you to get a 750 man army in your realm capital. Swell your Mangonels to the maximum size. Have your bishop fabricate a claim on any duchy within Epirus; the Byzantines will be weak due to the Seljuk invasion and Robert is an exceptional commander. After this, an event can appear where you support a monk's claim for the Byzantine throne in exchange for the Kingdom of Epirus. Start the war via the event. You will get 3000 special soldiers and will easily win if Robert is still alive. Winning this war gives tons of gold so don't bother waiting to create titles at the start. Convert all counties in Epirus to Christianity. (Note that in my experience, the relevant events will not fire while you are at war, so don't get into wars before they do.) The achievement does not require you to be independent, so it is also possible (if the Roads to Power DLC is NOT installed, making the Byzantines remain Feudal) to swear fealty to the Byzantine Empire, obtain the Religious Rights feudal contract via hook, and conquer and convert the required provinces as a vassal. Alternatively, educate your heir as Greek and Orthodox, claim the emperor's throne with him, and then convert back to Catholic with your vassals, taking care not to lose the Kingdom of Sicily, Naples, or Trinacria.

description_en: Have only two distinct parents, grandparents, and great grandparents, counting absent ancestors as individual Characters
requirements_en: Missing unique ancestors is 50 or more.
how_to_en: Does not require two distinct grandparents or great-grandparents. Requires having no more than 12 unique characters among the player's parents through great-great-great-grandparents (the maximum possible is 62; 62 − 50 = 12). If there is no recorded parent, as for lowborn, it's counted as a whole tree of unrelated ancestors. Start in Iceland with the restricted diplomatic range rule, where you're rather isolated and can be left alone. Use the Ruler Designer to create a character of the Messalianism faith with the Pure-blooded trait, then inbreed siblings for three generations. The greatest threat to this achievement is cuckoldry. Playing a female ruler (easy as Messalianism is gender-equal) together with the predominant Homosexuality or Asexuality rules can alleviate this. Alternatively, play as a Zoroastrian ruler somewhere in India. Since it is an Eastern Religion, you won't have to worry about Holy Wars. **This achievement is bugged in the current patch (as of April 2025). It will never trigger no matter what you do. Use the Steam Beta feature to roll your version back to 1.13.2 and it should trigger if you've met the requirements.

description_en: Starting as Magajiva Daura, reform an African pagan faith, and convert all Counties in Africa to it
requirements_en: Has reformed and converted all African Counties to one of the following religions: / Mandé Yoruba Hausan Senegambian Akan Kordofanian Oromo-Somali North-African
starting_conditions_en: Is playing Countess Daurama Daura of Kano in 867
how_to_en: The Prophet lifestyle perk would reduce the price of reforming faith by 50%. Reforming the Faith with the Fundamentalist doctrine and the Mendicant Preachers and Sanctioned False Conversions tenets to increase the realm's conversion speed and resistance. Warmonger or Reincarnation are good choices for the third tenet. Revocable Clergy appointment doctrine allows you to select a priest with higher learning to speed up manual conversion. Try not to overuse Holy Wars, for they decrease fervor and at 40% or less there can be a heresy outbreak (of the unreformed version of your faith). Ensure that all of the county holders in your realm within Africa are converted to the proper faith. Keep an eye on nearby cultures, especially new hybrids, and hybridize with one that gets you closer to feudalizing. If succession becomes an issue, consider adopting administrative government (if the Roads to Power DLC is installed). Succession will not split the realm as long as you keep one of your dynasty members at the top of the score. Use political schemes to achieve this. Remember: all of Africa has to follow your religion, not your faith. Those who follow the unreformed version of your faith are still following your religion.

description_en: As a dwarf, win a Varangian Adventurer War anywhere outside of Europe
requirements_en: Won a Varangian Adventurer Conquest war outside of Europe. Dwarf trait
how_to_en: In 867 create a Norse culture character with the Dwarf trait. Win a war with the Varangian Adventure casus belli. A simple method is to have your custom character start as the Duke of Småland, then after some raiding and hunting to get sufficient troops and prestige, launch the Varangian Adventure on the nomadic duchy title of Karayedisan, which is outside Europe and within diplomatic range. Alternatively, create a Norse character with the Dwarf trait anywhere on the map and target the nearest valid character. You will have to make do with your own troops (no special soldiers) this way but there are plenty of weak rulers in 867 to do this with.

description_en: Have your Dynasty erect a hundred runestones
requirements_en: Has taken the "Raise Runestone" decision 100 times.
how_to_en: The fastest method involves a game exploit. In 1066, replace the Seljuk Emperor with a custom ruler of Norse culture who is 46 years old, unmarried, and has one son. Put all points into Stewardship. On game start, assign good tax collectors and completely max out the Wealth tree in the Stewardship lifestyle focus. Wait ten years, making sure you build up to around 6000 gold, then wait for the Dissolution Faction to form. Surrender to them. Now as Persia, give away as many of your titles as you can, keeping one duchy title and at least three counties that are part of it. Surrender to the faction again when it pops. Now as a duke, land your son with one of the counties in your realm, and land a random noble with the other. Drive down the random vassal's opinion, then try and fail to imprison him. Surrender to the rebellion to be deposed. Choose to play as your son, then raise the runestone (gaining a rank from count to duke allows it to be raised), invite your father to court, land him, imprison the vassal, and repeat until the achievement pops. Don't spend unnecessary gold and keep your income low; the cost of runestones scales with income, with the minimum being 50. You will need at least 5000 gold to complete this. It is not recommended to try to achieve this via normal gameplay. The runestone decision has a 10 year cooldown except in cases of death or deposition. It will therefore take 1000 years to complete the achievement, which is not possible in normal gameplay. The achievement relies on extraordinary circumstances to fire naturally; using one of these exploits is the only reliable method.

requirements_en: Has North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Capital is on an island in the Indian Ocean: One of the following Duchies Within the Kingdom of Lanka: Dakhina Desa Ruhunu Sinhala Within the Kingdom of Yemen: Socotra One of the following Counties Within the Kingdom of Tamilakam: Maldives Within the Kingdom of Gujarat: Kutch
starting_conditions_en: North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Custom character
how_to_en: If you have the Roads to Power DLC, in 867 start as Master Ubbe of the Scourge of the Saxons. As an adventurer, you can easily move your camp down the the island of Socotra off the coast of Yemen, stopping to gather Provisions along the way. Your camp is considered your Realm Capital, so as soon as you make camp on the island, you should get the achievement. Without the DLC, in 867 start as Jarl Hæsteinn of Montaigu. Start of by taking counties from the North Africans, Tulunids, the East Africans, and finally use the Varangian Adventure casus belli on the island of Socotra.

description_en: Starting and staying as a North Germanic Ásatrúan of any kind, Completely Control the Kingdom of Sicily before 1047. May not be available for characters created in the Ruler Designer.
requirements_en: Has North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Completely controls the Kingdom of Sicily Is Independent Date is before 1047.1.1
starting_conditions_en: North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Custom character
how_to_en: Although the steam achievement states Norse tribal, the in-game achievement has been changed to allow any government type, including Jarl Haesteinn who is now feudal. This will still trigger the steam achievement. Starting as Jarl Haesteinn in 867 use the Duchy conquest casus belli to seize Sicily while they are at war with the Byzantines. Move your capital to Palermo and start conquering small independent Counts and Dukes. For Byzantine territories, wait for a weakness or make allies to match their strength and use the Kingdom conquest casus belli to take all of the land in one war, since it could be a costly war keep it for the end. Alternatively, after taking enough non-Byzantine and Italian land to create the Kingdom, swear fealty to the Byzantines and the emperor should grant you the remaining de-jure counties in the Kingdom of Sicily. Follow this with an independence war when having a stronge enough faction or enough allies. Another approach (if the Roads to Power DLC is NOT installed, which keeps the Byzantines Feudal) would be to swear fealty to the Byzantine Emperor after you have seized Sicily, start by conquering the smaller independent realms and vassals of the Byzantine empire. You should modify your feudal contract to protect yourself from title revocation, you can always add religious rights to your contract later. You will then have to break away from the empire eventually and can do this via an independence faction, use your spy master to find hooks on the various dukes in the empire so you can force them to join. A few assassinations can also destabilize the empire. Once you are independent the achievement should fire.

description_en: Starting and staying as a North Germanic Ásatrúan of any kind, Completely Control the Kingdom of Thessalonika & hold any Empire without holding Byzantine Empire. May not be available for characters created in the Ruler Designer.
requirements_en: Has North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Completely controls the Kingdom of Thessalonika Empire title Byzantine Empire
starting_conditions_en: North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru Custom character
how_to_en: Starting as Count Hæsteinn of Montaigu in 867, declare a conquest war on Count Gregorios of Napoli to reach the Diplomatic Range needed to declare on The Byzantine Empire. Hire mercenaries to fight for you to not waste your special soldiers, hire one company for the county war and save your money for later. If you capture any important couriers, make sure to ransom them for some extra money, which means extra soldiers. When you are ready, declare war on The Byzantine Empire with the Invade Kingdom casus belli and select Thessalonika. Preferably wait until The Byzantine Emprie is at war or deals with rebellions. Priortize sieging down counties close to the objective and preferably inside the objective. Eventually you will have to face the armies of The Byzantine Empire, defending will most times just about make you win, but getting them to cross a strait (for example between Kalliopolis and Lampsakos) will greatly increase your chances of winning. If you reset your perks at the start, you can chose to go the Gallant path. This will help with overal combat and decrese the war-score needed to win the war. You can now go on and create any empire you want. The Empire of Ajuraan only needs 18 counties and you can reach High Chiefdom of Naqis to not deal with The Tulunid Sultanate and The Abbasid Empire. Accept any Peasent Rabble's as it is way less trouble to have the lowered controll than dealing with the rebels, as you won't gain a lot of money from tax. Allying some big kingdoms with lots of men will help discourage The Byzantine Empire to retake provinces. Fortunately, most times they will implode.

description_en: Starting and staying as a North Germanic Ásatrúan of any kind, Completely Control everything in the Islands in the Atlantic and Mediterranean Region without ever having over 80 Realm Size
requirements_en: Is North Germanic culture group or Norman Asatru Realm Size above 80 provinces Completely controls the following: The following Duchies: Within the Kingdom of Norway:f Iceland Northern Isles Within the Kingdom of Denmark: Sjælland Within the Kingdom of Sweden: Visby Within the Kingdom of Scotland: the Isles Mann Within the Kingdom of Maghreb: Canarias Within the Kingdom of Valencia: Mallorca Within the Kingdom of Sardinia: Sardinia Corsica Within the Kingdom of Sicily: Sicily Within the Kingdom of Venice: Venice Within the Kingdom of Epirus: Cephalonia Within the Kingdom of Krete: Krete Within the Kingdom of Cyprus: Cyprus The following Counties: Within the Kingdom of Denmark: Bornholm Within the Kingdom of Sweden: Aland Öland Within the Kingdom of Estonia: Osel Within the Kingdom of Wales: Anglesey Within the Kingdom of England: Wight Within the Kingdom of Thessalonika: Chios Lesbos Naxos Rhodos
starting_conditions_en: North Germanic heritage or Norman culture Asatru
how_to_en: The most powerful island holder is the Byzantine Empire. Try starting as Jarl Haesteinn in 867 and take a part of Sardinia to get in range of them. Then immediately invade Thessalonika with mercenaries to capture both the islands in it and to take Constantinople away from them to weaken them for future wars. Form or usurp a duchy, but don't form the kingdom of Thessalonika yet as having a kingdom will block the Elevate the Kingdom of Mann & the Isles decision. Conquer weaker islands to build Haesteinn's fame to that of a Living Legend. Wait for Ivar the Boneless or his event spawn troops to die and take the Isle of Mann. Then use Elevate the Kingdom of Mann & the Isles decision to get a kingdom title and more event spawn troops. Use these troops to battle the powerful Karlings, Wessex, Arabs, and the Byzantines for the rest of the islands. In version 1.3.1 you can go over 80 realm size, for the achievement to unlock you will have to go under the 80 realm size and reload the save file. This is due to the game only checking if it is possible at the start of the game and does not (yet) add any flags to the game to fully disable it. After getting this achievement consider doing the Miklagarðaríki achievement in the same playthrough : the empire of Italia only needs 42 counties (you already have Corsica and Sardinia) and if the kingdom of Italy exists you can annex it in one war.

description_en: Personally know 10 Languages
requirements_en: Knows 10 languages
how_to_en: Create a 21 years old custom character with the Erudite Oracle trait and put the remaining points into Learning. Choose a culture with the "Astute Diplomats" tradition for the +1 foreign language limit and +25% Learn Language Scheme Power (Franconian, German, Nubian, Volhynian, Öngüd, Croatian, Bolghar) or with "Culture Blending" for the +30% Learn Language Scheme Power and +1 Max Learn Language Schemes (Mon, Gond, Kamrupi, Avar, Merya, Meshchera, Muroma). Use the free perks to get the Pedagogy and Open-Minded perks. Use the Learn Language scheme to learn more languages. Choose a diplomacy lifestyle and get the Adaptive Traditions perk for an extra scheme. You can also convert to a culture with a different language or hybridize with one to instantly learn its language.

description_en: Starting as the Piasts in 867, rule Poland, adopt Feudalism, and own a Famed or Illustrious-rarity regalia, crown, weapon, and armor.
requirements_en: Kingdom of Poland Government is Feudal Famed or illustrious armor Famed or illustrious crown Famed or illustrious regalia Famed or illustrious weapon
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Siemowit of Greater Poland in 867
how_to_en: The fastest way to adopt Feudalism is to use the "Adopt Feudal Ways through Liege" decision. Conquer land in Poland until you are stronger than the ruler of Great Moravia. Then border Great Moravia and convert to Catholicism so the leader will want to take you as a vassal. Swear fealty to Great Moravia and take the decision to become feudal. At this point either fight for independence to be able to form Poland or take over Great Moravia from within. Either way, make sure you own and keep the Kingdom of Poland. The artifacts can be acquired by sponsoring their creation yourself, and by taking them from others. To get more Inspired characters, raise court grandeur, switch to a Scholarly court, and get the first Erudition dynasty legacy. Periodically use the character finder to search for characters with inventory artifacts, sorting by rank, and focusing on emperors and kings. With the Accomplished Forger perk, buy claims on their artifacts, then demand the artifacts, steal them or go to war for them.

description_en: Have an Artifact in every slot in the Inventory and Royal Court
requirements_en: All Inventory artifact slots are used All Royal Court artifact slots are used
how_to_en: In your inventory you will need a crown, a journal, a weapon, armor, regalia, and 4 trinkets. The crown, weapon, armor, and regalia can all be commissioned. Trinkets can be obtained through natural gameplay. Successful hunts will often give you a trinket. Journals can be obtained through random events, the Create Travel Itinerary decision, and the Commission Epic decision. Both of these decisions are unlocked through perks, from Wandering and Diplomacy lifestyles respectively. In the Royal Court you will need 3 large wall ornaments, 3 small wall ornaments, 4 pedestal items, 2 furniture items, 2 books, and a throne. The throne is the hardest to obtain, so playing as Scotland in 1066 is a good idea as they start with one. Otherwise, a throne can sometimes be obtained by taking the Commission Artifact decision and picking furniture. The wall ornaments, furniture, and books can be commissioned directly, and the pedestal items can be obtained by reforging inventory artifacts.

description_en: As a member of the Pugyel Dynasty, re-create the Empire of Tibet
requirements_en: Empire of Tibet
starting_conditions_en: Starting as a member of the Pugyel dynasty
how_to_en: Achievable in a couple of decades as Chéchentsa Tsédé of Gugé in 1066. Restart until you get a Diplomacy education and respec perks to get True Ruler. Almost everything in the empire of Tibet can be diplomatically vassalized besides the kingdoms of Maryül and Mjinjaa. Claim Maryül as the dynasty head. To allow for more vassalization, sway the targets, and create the kingdoms that are de jure above them. Focus on wealth for faster title creation. Mjinjaa is not necessary to create the empire, but can be subjugated with a high enough level of fame; if this is the goal, prioritize prestige & fame instead of gold. Namdé Ösung of Gugé in 867 is also viable. Be reminded that the faith Namdé Ösung follows does have the tenet "Dharmic Pacifism" which disallows you to fight holy wars. Creating a new faith with "Warmonger" instead will allow you conquests and holy wars for easier blobbing.

description_en: Starting as the Duke of Ghur in 867 or 1066, conquer the Region of the Historical Ghurid Empire
requirements_en: Completely controls the Historical Ghurid Empire region: The following Duchies: Within the Kingdom of Bengal: Gauda Varendra Vanga Nadia Suhma Within the Kingdom of Bihar: Magadha Tirabhukti Kasi Within the Kingdom of Kosala: Kanyakubja Saryupara Within the Kingdom of Delhi: Kuru Haritanaka Mathura Vodamayutja Within the Kingdom of Punjab: Multan Lahore Gandhara Within the Kingdom of Rajputana: Maru Jangladesh Stravani Ajmer Within the Kingdom of Sindh: Sauvira Bhakkar Within the Kingdom of Makran: Makran Jabal Kufs Sistan Within the Kingdom of Kabulistan: Kabulistan Zabulistan Within the Kingdom of Persia: Kirman Within the Kingdom of Khorsan: Merv Ghur Herat Balkh The following Counties: Within the Kingdom of Khorsan: Nishapur Tus
starting_conditions_en: Starting as Firuzkuhshah Zahhak of Ghurid in 867 or Muhammad Abbaszde of Ghurid in 1066
how_to_en: You will need Muhammad Abbaszde (1066) for this to work. When choosing him, make sure to look at the traits, if you don't get something that boosts your stewardship and learning quit to main menu and look at him again. When satisfied with traits, replace his liege with a female character that has all the sinful traits and ones that damage fertility, make sure to decrease her skills as low as possible. Now you may start the game. Look at your education, if it isn't stewardship - repeat the previous steps until you get it. Reroll the perks, unlock the Meritocracy perk (put the rest towards avaricious then switch to learning for the whole of body trait, if you get lucky you'll be able to do the achievement in one lifetime) and start the claim throne scheme against your liege. Once you start your claimant faction peasants will join you and she will give up the throne without fight. Kill her to inherit her titles. Now you should completely control the duchies of Ghur and Kabulistan. Fill the building slots with money making buildings and start filling out men at arms (with at least two regiments of mangonels) Swear fealty to the Seljuk empire and use the claim throne scheme again. Forge alliances through marriage and start a claimant faction/declare war for your claim. Naturally, your best bet would be to wait for the emperor to go to war. Siege you way to the capital one county at a time. His forces should run out of supply by the time they reach you, so your first fight should be easy even without your allies. Once you win the war, proceed to conquer the required territories as you normally would. Consider converting to Afghan culture, they have a tradition that allows the use of tribal elective law. Apply it to the two duchies and steer house unity towards antagonistic for the infinite invasion CB

description_en: Have all Royal Courts in the world speak your Court Language. May not be available for characters created in the Ruler Designer.
requirements_en: Royal Court All Royal Courts have the same court language
starting_conditions_en: Custom character
how_to_en: No custom characters allowed. The most straight forward way to accomplish this is to destroy all royal courts in the game other than your own. Fast kingdom conquest can be done with the By The Sword tradition, which removes the once per lifetime restriction on kingdom level holy wars. AI Adoption of another court's language is weighted down by being a different religious group and their court capital being outside of the same geographic region, and against the number of counties with the AI's current court language. This means that an AI whose religious group is different from yours or whose capital is outside of your capital's region will not adopt your court's language. To keep a vassal king from changing culture to the local culture, or change an existing vassal king's culture, you can convert the culture of their capital county. The AI will switch culture to their liege's if their capital county is the same culture as their liege's. However, the AI is biased towards keeping their current court language. If after sometime the AI doesn't switch their court language to yours, revoke the kingdom title and give it to a character of your culture. Consider starting as Charles the Bald in 867. You start with claims on three additional kingdoms held by your much weaker relatives, and once conquered you will be in a great position to take the "Restore Carolingian Borders" decision giving you a huge de jure empire (and more importantly, the Primogeniture succession law) very early on. From there, just have fun conquering!

description_en: With the County of Fika as your Primary Title, diverge your Culture and spread it to 30 Counties held by you or your Vassals
requirements_en: Has created a divergent culture At least 30 counties in the realm have the same culture
how_to_en: Note: The County of Fika has to be your primary title at the time you diverge your culture. After that you do not need to have the County of Fika as your primary title anymore to get the achievement. Disable AI culture hybridization and divergence in the game rules to prevent fracture of your culture by your vassals. Pick on your weaker neighbors and diverge your culture as soon as you have the prestige, then form a duchy and just play a regular game, converting enough counties to get the achievement. Consider swearing fealty to the Duke of Kanem right away for safety and ease of expansion, since he'll create a kingdom soon after, allowing you to create a duchy yourself. Converting to the Coptic faith lets you promote your culture 50% faster in converted counties thanks to the Communal Identity tenet. Or simply reform Bori to have that tenet. Alternatively, start as Jarl Haesteinn in 867. Immediately assign your steward to convert the County of Montaigu to Norse culture and your Godi (Realm Priest) to fabricate a claim on the County of Fika. In the mean time, use the "Conquer County" Casus Belli for the County of Melilla to secure a foothold in Africa. Next, use the "Conquer County" Casus Belli to take the County of Zammour, which grants the diplomatic range to declare war on coastal Sub-Saharan holdings. Declare another "Conquer County" war for any Sub-Saharan coastal holding of your choosing, but be careful not to lose Haesteinn's special event troops to attrition. You may now enforce your claim on the County of Fika. After taking the County of Fika and converting the County of Montaigu to Norse culture, select the title "County of Fika," and make it your primary title. Diverge the Norse culture. Once diverged, Varangian Adventure for a title of your choosing or continue as you are and complete the achievement by converting 30 counties to your culture.

description_en: End the Iberian Struggle through the Status Quo Ending
requirements_en: Achieved the Status Quo ending for the Iberian Struggle
how_to_en: Requires being Involved in the struggle (not Interloper). Keep constructing buildings that provide taxes in city holdings to push the struggle phase towards Conciliation, then do the same in castle holdings to reach the Compromise phase. Then grant independence to vassals until you control less than 50% of Iberia. Make sure that you keep all holdings in your primary kingdom title when releasing vassals, as that is one of the requirements. Releasing one or two large vassals to go under the 50% limit will make it easier to meet the requirement of having 60 opinion or strong hooks on all independent involved rulers

After winning the war give your starting domain to someone else and move your capital to the newly conquered domain. Give the head of faith title to someone else so you can convert to Muwalladism. Wait for the struggle phase to reach Compromise and pick the Status Quo ending. Alternatively, play as Eudes Robertine of Anjou in 867, working towards the Kings to the Seventh Generation achivement. As King of France or Emperor of Francia it is very easy to conquer Iberian land as nobody will be powerful enough to stop you. Conquer what you need, convert to Mozarabism, and end the struggle however you like. You can get three achievements in one run this way, at minimum.

how_to_en: Once a (non-legendary) hunt starts, choose to target the lynx, bear, leopard, lion, or tiger, then choose to corner it (not ambush with bow, etc.), then choose to capture it. Without going into great detail, big cats are easier to find in African desert or Indian jungle; lynxes and bears in Europe, the steppe and Tibet. While targeting any animal, it's also possible to randomly encounter a wolf, hyena, or any of the aforesaid five animals, and with very high diplomacy, choose to befriend the animal, which will capture it if successful. Wolves and hyenas count for the achievement in the random event, but there is no option to capture them when they are intentionally targeted. The Slay Beast intent does not prevent capture opportunities.

how_to_en: The Joust event requires the Horseshoes and Arched Saddle innovations and either a capital in the Europe region, the Chivalry tradition, or the Chanson de Geste tradition. Perks in the Strategist and Gallant trees give Hastiluder experience gain. The fifth Activities legacy increases Hastiluder experience gain. Traveling gives a little Horse experience based on distance traveled. You can't have a Joust in mountains or wetlands. Your character has to be the one with the Horse track; a champion won't count.

description_en: Starting as the Habsburgs, rule the Archduchy of Austria, arrange 5+ Grand Weddings, and don’t wage War
requirements_en: Rules the Archduchy of Austria Arranged at least 5 Grand Weddings Declared any war
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Werner of Aargau in 1066 or Albrecht of Aargau in 1178
how_to_en: Diplomacy Path: With diplomacy and piety, you can get duchy claims from the pope, petition your liege for the titles (keep liege opinion high), and rely on allies to win wars to revoke counties for a better domain. You can't petition for titles if your liege has the lowest crown authority. Alternately, become emperor and revoke or create the duchies, and ensure none are lost on succession while you lose the empire. Make sure no one from the Etichonen dynasty is elected as the emperor. Intrigue Path: Start as Werner in Aargau, marry your son into the duchy of Austria, and become an intrigue-focused character. Murder the two sons who would inherit instead. Then, as you receive the duchy with your grandchildren, use your bishop to constantly get claims on the Steyermark duchy and petition for it from your liege. Or try to marry into the family and once again get your heir to inherit both titles. Grand Weddings have a chance to give you a strong hook on your liege, which you need for the Archduchy of Austria decision, and the first Dynasty legacy of 'Activities' increases that chance (make sure the emperor isn't busy and will attend when planning the weddings). Alternatively, you can get the "Ritualized Friendship" tradition for your culture, and Swear Oath of True Friendship with the Emperor. Strong hooks can also sometimes be fabricated. All wars where you are not the primary aggressor (the character who declared the war) are allowed, including tyranny wars from imprisoning or revoking, factions against you, crusades, or ally's wars.

description_en: Have a max rank Acclaimed Knight with the Warrior of the Faith Trait
requirements_en: Has a max rank Accolade with the Crusader, Mujahid or Warrior of the Faith trait
how_to_en: Acclaimed knights gain glory per winning battle they participate in, so farming as many battles as possible through constant wars and raiding is the fastest way to reach level 6. Norse is highly recommended as they have a +20% glory bonus through cultural traditions and faith tenets, and can raid overseas to constantly fight battles without waiting to siege wargoals. The most straightforward path for this achievement is to start as any Norse ruler in 867 and play a regular game. Create the accolade one you're a King and rconstantly raid overseas with the Adventure raid intent The side effect of this wis that you will hav bountiful wealth that you can use to keep expanding. Option 1 - When powerful enough, conquer Rome from the pope to force Christianity into unlocking Crusades, then convert. A Crusade should be called for Jerusalem very quickly. Participate and send your armies to the war goal to give your knight the trait and get the achievement. Ensure that the Accolade always has a successor, or it will lose rank. Option 2 - Build up enough piety from pilgrimages to form a new religion. It will need the Theocratic Clerical Tradition and Temporal Head of Faith (which will be you) doctrines, and the Armed Pilgra tenant. Then pick a victim, and declare your own Great Holy War . Note that you do not need to win the war, but your acclaimed knight must enter the target kingdom and fight there (sieges or battles) to get the trait.

description_en: Pass through every Terrain, including naval, whilst on the same Travel
requirements_en: Passed through every terrain while traveling during an activity
how_to_en: Create a custom character with the Pilgrim trait. When planning the activity manually add detours through each terrain type. Detours can't deviate from the optimal path too much, with a limit on additional travel time, so starting from taiga in the north or jungle in the south and ending on the opposite side of the map, while passing through Egypt for floodplains and oasis, is the easiest to plan. The two naval terrains are Sea and Coastal Sea. Example: Start as the Chiefdom of Obran Osh in 867 with Vaishnavism (Hindu) as your faith and perform a pilgrimage to Ujjayini with the following stops: Obran Osh - Start Theodosia Alexandria Ujjayini - Destination Ghatagaon Rajpipla Baqirda This route will hit the 15 terrains on land and 2 at sea, at least once at: Obran Osh - Taiga Mtensk - Forest Chally-Kala - Steppe Or Qapi - Wetlands Theodosia - Mountains Black Sea - Coastal Sea Levantine Sea - Sea Alexandria - Oasis Cairo - Floodplains Quzlam - Desert Kamboika - Plains Ujjayini - Farmlands Rajpipla - Jungle Basiyan - Drylands Baqirda - Desert Mountains Saird - Hills Ghatagaon - Terraced Hills

how_to_en: Travel until you acquire the Traveler trait. The Wanderer track is easily leveled by travelling a lot. The Seasoned track requires encountering Danger events while traveling, so ensure your Caravan Master and travel options do not make travel Safety too high, and then plan travels which make detours through provinces with appreciable Danger. It's possible to modify the route while the travel is ongoing to make further detours towards short-lived dangers like battles and sieges. Choosing the safest options in Danger events may lead to reduced or zero Seasoned experience.

description_en: Have a capital Barony with 60+ Income, a 500+ Stationed Heavy Cavalry Regiment, and a mine
requirements_en: Has a capital Barony with 60+ Income, a 500+ size stationed Heavy Cavalry unit and a Special mine
how_to_en: There are 27 special building slots where mines can be built, but some are better than others. Mines have a unique mine icon on the Economy map mode. Considering terrain for buildings (for windmills, etc.), the best mine counties are Bambuk in Mali, Göttingen in Germany, Mudigonda in Andhra, Malaya in Lanka, and Bure in Mali. The ones with desert/dry terrain are the worst. Change the county capital to the holding with the mine if necessary. The barony's income is indicated as "Tax" in the Holding view. "Monthly Income" modifiers like Greedy or Wealth Focus are irrelevant. Increase "Taxes" with buildings, high personal stewardship, Tax Offices in the duchy building slot (+15-30%), increasing Development, and Collecting Taxes with a skilled steward. You can get the Avaricious trait (+15%) and the Absolute Control perk (+10%), and be the Steward of your liege (+10% if emperor). A common event while traveling through another ruler's province with the Cattle Pastures building can give the Improved Pastures modifier (+20%) to your capital. The Domain Focus has many random events which may give holding tax modifiers. With a good county, this is possible with High Medieval tech, and trivial with Late Medieval tech.

description_en: As a Regent, successfully Coup your Liege and take their Realm without War or Schemes
requirements_en: As a Regent, successfully Coup your Liege and take their Realm without War or Schemes
how_to_en: In 1066 the Fatimid Sultanate starts with a regency at 100 scales of power towards the regent. The Emir of Alexandria is the most powerful and wealthy vassal. Use the Overthrow Regent scheme on the Fatimid regent to take the regency for yourself. The Fatimid Sultanate is prone to dissolving by faction, so create or join the dissolution faction yourself so that you can prevent it from firing. Use ruler designer to create a genius ruler with high overall stats and marry a 20+ skill wife that matches the skill of the regency mandate (Fill Coffers = Stewardship, Swell Armies = Martial, Promote Authority = Diplomacy). You will need to succeed regularly at the mandate events, in addition to using the Swings the Scales decision, to maintain Scales of Power level 6. Given that the scales have a natural decay every year, and your liege could use the Swings the Scales decision as well, balancing your swinging, you heavily rely on the random outcome of the mandate events, making it really hard to maintain level 6. However, once you kidnap your liege, he can't swing the scales in jail, just release him and coup after the other conditions are met.

description_en: Take the Dominate the Caliph foundation as part of the Abbasid Humiliation ending in the Iranian Intermezzo Struggle
requirements_en: Achieved the Abbasid Humiliation - Dominate the Caliph ending for the Iranian Intermezzo
how_to_en: Holder of the Sunni Caliphate must also hold the Arabian Empire. Start as Amiran Ya'qub of Makran or replace him with a custom ruler if you wish. Win the starting war and conquer as many of the independent realms as you can. Steer your House Unity towards Antagonistic, allowing unlimited use of the Subjugate Kingdom Casus Belli, then use it to conquer Mesopotamia and Jazira. The Levant is very dense with counties so you will need to focus on taking as many of them away from the Caliph as possible, simply trying to outcompete him in conquest will be challenging. You must personally hold (cannot be held by a vassal) the County of Samarra for five years to take the decision. After conquering Mesopotamia you will have all of the territorial requirements, simply conquer until you're bigger than the Empire and take the decision once the five years are up.

description_en: Loot four Illustrious-tier Vizier Extravagance modifiers from your Vizier
requirements_en: Looted four Illustrious-tier Vizier Extravagance modifiers from the Vizier
how_to_en: Appoint a vizier by right clicking on one of your tax collectors, only available if you are at peace. There are four tiers of modifiers: First tier : Notable, the Vizier does a check every 12-18 months for 100 gold Second tier : Remarkable, same but for 200 gold Third tier : Famous, 300 gold Fourth tier : Illustrious, 400 gold One modifier will be gained at a time, and the vizier will only start to gain Remarkable modifiers once they have four Notable modifiers, etc. This means it will take about 16 years to build up four Illustrious modifiers. This is more reliable if you ensure your vizier always has more than 400 gold by sending gifts intermittently. Once all four are present, right click your vizier. Go for Mulct Vizier and Aggressive Auditing. If your vizier dies, you will have to restart therefore make sure the vizier can sustain those 16 years. If you die the stacks don't disappear.

requirements_en: Iranian heritage Empire of Persia Kingdom Thessalonika Kingdom of Hellas
how_to_en: Start as the Seljuk Emperor in 1066. You should win the ongoing war easily. Try to limit the usage of the special troops since those don't respawn. Go in pilgrimages to get the necessary level of devotion for Kingdom-level holy wars. At the same time start a murder plot to eliminate the Byzantine Emperor to avoid the truce cooldown. Declare a holy war for Pontus. Kill the Byzantine Emperor and declare duchy wars for the actual targets. You can also wait for a rebellion to remove the Byzantine Emperor and the truce cooldown will disappear as well. N.B. : Remember to convert to a culture of Iranian heritage. You do not need every county in all 3 titles for the achievement to trigger, but you need to own the kingdoms yourself as opposed to being the liege of the owner.

description_en: Starting as a member of the Umayyad dynasty, hold the Empire of Arabia and the Sunni Caliphate
requirements_en: Empire of Arabia Sunni Caliphate
starting_conditions_en: Dynasty is Umayyad
how_to_en: The Caliphate will be destroyed if the holder loses all of his land. Starting as al-Andalus in 867, conquer the weaker duchies on the African coast, then use them to take Krete, allowing you to swear fealty to the Abbasids. Use the Claim Throne scheme to take the empire, then let the Caliphate be independent after you become the emperor. Prestige is not required for the decision to restore the Caliphate, so you can ignore the truce and conquer all of the available land. Make sure to get piety level 3 before doing so, otherwise another character can create the Caliphate title before you. Then, simply take the decision to restore the Caliphate. Alternately, use the game rule to make the Abbasids Administrative. Then, if you take the throne, you will simply gain the Caliphate title at the same time.

requirements_en: Title rank is Count Completed a legend of Mythical quality
how_to_en: In game rules, set Legend Spread Chance to Very High and Legend Cost to Very Low. In 867, replace the Chief of Gotland in Scandinavia with a custom ruler that has high stewardship and the Holy Monarch trait. This trait unlocks a legend seed that has no fame or piety requirements. Ask every neighboring ruler to promote it as often as you can and focus on saving up gold to petition more rulers. Gotland is fairly safe from its larger neighbors and you can ask every Norse ruler to promote your legend. Consider conquering land on the Baltic coast to unlock more possible rulers for promotion. Another option is to start and promote the legend as a King or Emperor, then wait until death, choose a new destiny as a count, take ownership of the legend from your deceased dyansty member, and complete it.

how_to_en: In game rules set Plague Frequency to Doomsday and allow Black Death to happen at any point. Create a custom character with the Herculean/Amazonian, Strong, Temperate and Conscientious Scribe traits. Take the Medicine focus and the Whole of Body perks and always employ the best Personal Physician you can. Once a Bubonic Plague appears travel to the infected baronies. Catching the plague can be tricky, raising an army with your character as general and stationing it in an infected barony give you the highest chance to get it. The Personal Physician medical treatment event must happen within 1 year after getting Bubonic Plague. If you never get it, just fire and rehire them. When the event takes place, choose the risky treatment option. Losing the bubonic trait naturally will also trigger the achievement.

description_en: As a Legitimist Adventurer, win a claim war for a Kingdom or Empire.
requirements_en: Won a claim war as a Legitimist Adventurer
how_to_en: A fast, easy start for this is Master Todor, landless in 1178. He starts with 10k special troops, high gold and high prestige, and is aged only 24. You can become legitimist by buying a claim on an Orthodox state title from the Patriarch in Byzantium. His starting special troops and a few bought/requested men-at-arms should be sufficient to capture Tamar's Georgia or even a beleaguered Byzantine Empire without outside support. Other recommended starts are to accept defeat as King Harold (Fate of England, 1066) or un-land yourself as Prince Pascueten of Brittany (867). This strategy relies either on hoarding gold for mercenaries, or using the "Request Legitimist Support" interaction with a neighboring King (try Scotland/France). Note that you MUST be at least Exalted Among Men to receive troops and gold from the "Legitimist Support" contract; you can take the interaction before, but the contract will disappear if you travel for any other work.

name_en: It's OK, I Got a Permit
description_en: As a non-Powerful family, buy a Remit and successfully Raid the Estate of a Powerful Family.
requirements_en: Has a remit Completed a Raid Estate scheme Scheme target is part of a Powerful Family Powerful Family
how_to_en: The remit has a tendency to disappear without warning or feedback, so the safe approach is to start the raid scheme first, ask for the remit once it's ready to fire, and fire it as soon as you have the remit. Start in 1066 as Basileus Konstantinos X will almost always agree to grant a remit if you offer some gold. Begin the scheme as soon as possible as the remit will expire if the emperor dies. Try to start as a vassal who has a good traits for Diplomacy or Liege opinion, as this will make it more likely that Konstantinos will like you enough to give you the remit with a bribe. Strategos Iosephos of Kilikia Provence starts with a decent level of intrigue learning as an Intricate Webweaver which makes the scheme much easier. Make sure you have a decent spymaster and set them to support schemes. Try to pick an estate from a powerful family that doesn't have a guardhouse upgrade to improve your chances further.

description_en: In 1178, start as the Palaiologos and become Emperor of Byzantium.
requirements_en: Byzantine Empire Playing as Strategos Alexios or Tourmarchēs Alexios
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Strategos Alexios or Tourmarchēs Alexios in 1178
how_to_en: It needs to be accomplished in Alexios's lifetime. Start with the Skullduggery focus of the Intrigue Lifestyle and immediately begin scheming to murder the top candidate to the Empire. At the same time, use political schemes to promote yourself and slander others...the Challenge Status scheme is not recommended as it is very slow and takes up your political scheme slot. Use governance issues to gain influence, and gift gold to the heads of powerful families for even more influence. Use that influence to hurt other successors and promote yourself. It is likely that the emperor at game start will die before you get yourself into the top position. Don't worry, just keep building your score. Only murder candidates that are hundreds of score above yourself, as it is very quick and easy to slander people/hurt their candidacy through influence. Just keep a good watch on the succession window and continually start political schemes. You should easily get yourself to the top of the line of succession. After that, just wait for the emperor to die. Note: The achievement will be blocked if your Palaiologos successor is female. Note: available without Roads to Power DLC.

name_en: Started from the Bottom now we're ERE
description_en: As an adventurer, buy an Estate in Byzantium and lead your line to become Emperors.
requirements_en: Purchased an estate Byzantine Empire
starting_conditions_en: Adventurer
how_to_en: With minimal DLC, a strong start is Master Todor (1178, Landless Adventurer), who starts within Byzantine Empire borders with high gold, high prestige and a prestige gain modifier. Note that you will lose Todor's powerful special troops upon purchasing your estate, so feel free to throw them at the many enemies of the Empire. With the Khans of the Steppe DLC, however, starting in 1178 is NOT recommended because the Mongols will conquer rapidly, potentially un-landing you and leaving you rivaled with the Khan (making it tedious to acquire de jure land within or restore the Byzantine Empire title). An alternative start is Robert Phrangopoulos ("The Wandering Exiles", 1066), whose Martial skill and Prowess make for entertaining Swords-for-Hire play. Regardless of the chosen character and start date, the paths to the throne are about the same. Your first goal is to grow your family. You will need adult heirs to maintain your governorship and spread your dynasty. Marry a camp follower, romance nearby eligible courtiers, or complete "Rescue Fair Subject" contracts for a chance to invite a beautiful maiden to camp. Before landing yourself, your next goal is to buy an estate. Prioritize completing contracts for duchy-level patrons in the Empire, all of whom can be approached for the "Procure Estate" interaction. You will require 1000 gold, and either a weak hook, high prestige or high piety. You may also need to sway your target lord and improve your Level of Fame before (s)he agrees to grant the estate. While it is not necessary to get the Emperor as a patron, a hook may be useful later; look for "Fight Corruption" tasks, or decline an invitation to war so you can negotiate patronage. Once estate-landed, there are several Roads to Power. Stockpile Influence and seek candidacy for your House within the realm. If the Emperor likes you, you may be able to use 200+ Influence to request a governorship outright. You can also "Influence Candidacy" of yourself or adult heirs to other Themes besides your own. You can "Start Boundary Disputes" against neighboring vassals (preferably those belonging to Powerful Families) to take more land. Provided you first have a Theme, you may use the Meritocracy perk to make a claim on the Empire. Consider also kidnapping or murdering the Emperor to get your way. Alternatively, request the Emperor install you as Co-Emperor, and use the "Depose" scheme to win a bloodless coup.

description_en: Starting in 1178, take the Decision to Re-Establish the Theodosian Borders.
requirements_en: Taken the Re-Establish the Theodosian Borders decision
starting_conditions_en: Year is 1178
how_to_en: In 1178, play as Salāh al-Din of Ayyubid. Immediately declare a holy war for Jerusalem; you should win this war with ease. The decision requires that you control the Kingdoms of Syria, Jerusalem, and Egypt, the duchy of Diyarmudar in Jazira, the duchies of Amman and Shammar in Arabia, and the duchy of Syrte in Africa, in addition to every Byzantine kingdom except Serbia, Croatia, Armenia, and Georgia. Continue conquering the required land while building up piety (hold off on targeting Rum for now, a single de jure war can take them later), then convert to a Christian faith of your choosing, swear fealty to the Byzantine Empire, and adopt Administrative government. You can now focus on becoming emperor. Saladin's stats are very good, so you should have little trouble promoting yourself and slandering others to get yourself to the top of the succession. Once the emperor dies, seize whatever land you didn't already conquer and take the decision. Note: not available without Roads to Power DLC (due to requirement to hold an estate).

name_en: Rags to Riches to Rags to Riches
description_en: Starting as a Count, rule an Empire, become an Adventurer, then rule an Empire again.
requirements_en: Became an emperor, then became an adventurer, then became an emperor again
starting_conditions_en: Count
how_to_en: Can be done over a single or multiple characters but might not be awarded if other titles are held inbetween. In 867 start as a custom Baron Vassal of the Khagan of Khazaria at age 44 with Conniving Puppetmaster Education Trait, Sadistic and Deceitful Personality Traits, high Intrigue and a single Son. Increase your Authority to Limited Tribal Authority, Select a Stewardship Lifestyle and all Perks from the Schemer Perk Tree then wait until you receive a Stewardship Perk to get the Meritocracy Perk. Start a Claim Throne and Abduct Schemes against the Khagan. Do not complete the Abduct Schme before you have the Claim or the Scheme will be aborted. Marry your son to an infertile (age 46+) woman to his liking to ensure there won't be another Heir. As soon as you have the Claim also execute the Abduct Scheme, start a Claimant Faction and when you imprison the Khagan press your Claim. Enforce your Demands to become the Khagan and thus Emperor. With Limited Tribal Authority you can attempt to revoke a Title of a Vassal. Select one who provides 0% chance of success and will start a War against your Tyranny when they decline. Make sure you surrender to all other ongoing Wars first. You can't lose your Title while your fighting a War against a foreign Ruler. Surrender to your Vassals to get desposed and thus an Adventurer. Your Son will have become the new Khagan. Start a Murder Scheme against him. On successful execution of the Scheme you will be Khagan again and should thus receive the Achievement after at most a few ingame days.

starting_conditions_en: Playing as King Baudouin IV of Jerusalem in 1178
how_to_en: In game rules set Conquerors: Frequency to None. This way, Saladin will not have the conqueror trait. Jerusalem starts with higher income than the Ayyubids and Saladin is prone to moving west. At the start, fill your men-at-arms as high as possible (focus on crossbowmen) while simultaneously marrying off your sister to a powerful ally-Hungary and Georgia are both options if you can get the marriage to pop. Declare war on an adjacent county to your borders and wait for your ally to arrive. You should easily beat Saladin's army with their help.

description_en: As Princess Tamar, rule all of Georgia and fully conquer Armenia and Daylam.
requirements_en: Playing as Princess Tamar Completely controls the Kingdom of Armenia Completely controls the Kingdom of Daylam Completely controls the Kingdom of Georgia
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Princess Tamar in 1178
how_to_en: Play with the Gender Equality game rule set to Inverted. King Giorgi III starts with poor health and will eventually die, passing the kingdom of Georgia to you. Must be done within the lifetime of Princess Tamar. It is possible to start as King Giorgi III. When he dies, the achievement will become available for Princess Tamar. You can grant her a duchy to get her started while you proceed to conquer. Prioritise the territories that are most likely to fall to big rulers first, as fighting them later can be tough. The best Kingdom to focus on the soonest is Armenia as it is in danger of falling to the Byzantines, Abassids or the Sultanate or Rum. Pikeman have a natural advantage in the terrain so use mountains and hills to your advantage. Sieging will be more difficult especially in the mountainous region with higher fort levels, so make sure you have a full stack of trebuchets. Pick allies that are close to you and relatively stable. Hungary and the Byzantine Empire are normally good choices. Be aware that the Mongol horde are quickly approaching if their start date is set to historical in the game settings. It is possible to swear fealty to them and complete the achievement as long as you completely control the three kingdoms before Tamar dies.

description_en: Complete Hasan Sabbah unique content by destroying an Empire.
requirements_en: Playing as Hasan Sabbah Won the final war
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Hasan Sabbah in 1066
how_to_en: Hasan's story has a hidden tracker that advances during certain story events or after using the Evangelize the Faith decision. Stay in Persia, build up your piety (via pilgrimages, using the Prolific Pilgrims perk in the learning tree) and army, but don't take the decision to evangelize. Eventually you get called to Cairo. Once there, continue to build up an army (as large as possible) and wait for the Egyptian Caliph to die; this triggers another story event that allows you to convert to Nizarism. Always choose options that say "advance you down the path." Eventually another event will appear which will send you back to Persia; now it is time to Evangelize as much as possible, as you will recieve special soldiers scaling to the number of counties of your faith in the realm. At this point it is a good idea to wait for the Persian Emperor to die as his successor will be much weaker. Do not select the current Emperor as the enemy of the faith when prompted. Decline it until the young emperor becomes your option. Once this happens, found the Assassins and wait for civil wars to happen. You can then begin the revolution, which will typically result in an easy victory as the emperor deals with rebels. You can continue to evangelize or spread the Assassins if you wish, to make things easier. Must be completed during the lifetime of Hasan Sabbah.

requirements_en: Migrated to the Western Steppe
starting_conditions_en: Nomadic government Realm is in the Eastern Steppe
how_to_en: You can change the size of your horde for the migration target. There should always be herder counties to migrate to, so you can shrink from a higher title (duchy and above) to a county-level horde that only occupies one county at the end of the migration. Do this if you want to migrate fast and don't want to deal with wars, especially with confederations, as that could easily mean 10k~100k "coalitions" to deal with. Also, the Khazars could be a pain to deal with, since they can occupy most of the western steppe, but there usually should be at least one county be either independent or herder. The only other concern is gold and the army. If you have too many MAAs, you could have negative income after shrinking from a duke to a count, yet you still need a good army to deal with enemies. Excecise good judgement in balancing the two.

description_en: Become the Greatest of Khans.
requirements_en: Greatest of Khans trait
how_to_en: The Greatest of Khans trait is given upon winning the Great War of Defiance which will trigger in response to taking the Become the Greatest of Khans Major Nomadic Decision. As this is also on the path to take for the The Stallion that Mounts the World achievement starting as Temüjin is advisable but it is doable with any Nomadic Mongolian ruler with high Martial, Diplomacy and Prowess. A high Martial Education, the Military Engineer and Organizer Traits are also desirable. Set your Kurultai to Increase Control if necessary and to Support Herders or Manage Fertility otherwise. If you need Piety a Pilgrimage is more effective than Aid Stargazer. Make sure you have competent people in your Caravan Master, Noyan and Keeper of the Horses Court Positions and set their tasks to Prepare Armies, Cow Vassals and Warhorse Grooming. Do a Nerge Hunt each year (Hunt Activity cooldown is reduced by 50% because of Prolific Hunters Cultural Tradition) with Substantial Party Size Activity Option. Always pick the options on maxing out the success scoring first to get an Excellent Nerge Outcome.This is your main source of Gold, Prestige and Legitimacy. Nomadic rulers can move their Realm Capital as often as they want so you can move it close to the activity destination to have less travel time. Exclude Vassal and Tributary Guests to reduce how long you have to wait until it starts. Migrate to more fertile lands if you have done a Nerge Hunt in all of your realm counties. Create Men-at-Arms and improve your Realm Capital. Focus on Heavy Horse Archers and Torch Bearers Regiments as well as Domicile Buildings increasing Herd Capacity, Herd Gain, County Fertility and Army Movement Speed. Inbetween Nerge Hunts you can wage war with the Nomadic Quarrels, Bring under Tribute and Expand Dominance Casus Belli to grow your realm. Continue to Demand Vassalization and Tributaries, change Contracts to higher Herd Tithes, Demand Submission, Demand Horses and give Vassal Directives to increase County Fertility. Do not accept Vassals too remote from Mongolia to keep your Realm compact and avoid easy occupation targets for your war opponents. Offering to join wars on Tributaries defending against rulers from other Realms, hosting a Tsagaan Sar Feast, Funeral or Grand Tournament are also good ways to collect Prestige and opinion. Raiding and Stealing Herd can also increase your resources but will reduce opinion so preferably ignore those options. Continue until you can increase your Dominance and take Dominate Title and ultimately the Become the Greatest of Khans decisions. The ensuing Wars can have a Battle Won Score which is capped at 100% so focus on defeating the opposing hordes while you capture two or three holdings of them with a separate army. Opposing armies will attack your realm from multiple angles and attempt to retreat when they notice your army approaching which is why your Army Movement Speed is so important. Pick Strategist Perks and Nomadic Dynasty Legacies as they become available. After you've fully unlocked the Strategist Perk Tree switch to Diplomacy Lifestile Focus and aim for the True Ruler Perk. If you want the helpful Paiza System change the Cultural Fascination to Ledger as soon as you become the Cultural Head and if you have enough Prestige to spare reform Mongol by establishing the Horse Breeders (Warfare) and later on the Iron Cavalry (Regional) Cultural Traditions. Make sure to not to accidentaly take the Move into Conquered Lands, Settle the Steppe, Forsake Nomadism or Convert to Local Culture Decisions.

name_en: The Stallion that Mounts the World
description_en: Starting as Temüjin, conquer the world.
requirements_en: Control the entire world
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Temüjin in 1178
how_to_en: Follow the Guide to the This Chinggis Everything achievement first. Destroy your current Regiments and replace them with Keshig and Cataphract Archers recruited from your subjects. You should get access to those by having Khitan or Tangut Vassals. Use the Offer Submission or Ruin interaction on your neighbouring rulers even if the Acceptance Chance is 0%. Either Demand Siege Weapons if their culture has Trebuchet Innovation unlocked or Demand Special Soldiers if not. You can do this even with already raised armies so you can continue from one ruler to the next. Grant conquered Counties and Duchies to people of the local culture or Mongols with high Martial. Demand Submission and Demand or Inspire Conversion with all your new Vassals. Don't Demand Vassalization anymore. The Offer Submission or Ruin interaction is much more effective and can even be made on your Tributaries. Continue until you control the entire world.

description_en: Starting as a Wanua ruler, adopt Mandala government through your Suzerain, and build your first Capital Complex
requirements_en: Adopted Mandala government through suzerain Built a Capital Complex
starting_conditions_en: Wanua government
how_to_en: Starting as Raja Indra Warmandewa of Kutai in 867, immediately submit to Raja Aditya of Tanjungpura as a tributary and take the decision (which should be immediately available) to adopt Mandala government. To unlock the Complex Great Project (found in the Projects tab of the Decisions menu), you need a Legitimacy of 1, Level of Devotion of 2, and at least one Tributary (which you can easily get from wars or the Embrace as Tributary interaction). From here, all you need to do is gain Gold, Piety, and Legitimacy, the latter two coming via Mandala Rituals on the map and the Pilgrimage activity, and the former coming from the buildings you have right off the bat (you don't need much). Once you have enough, fund the Great Project and wait 5 years for it to be completed to get the achievement. The requirements for the Rite of Worthiness will change depending on your Mandala Aspect. Destruction is the fastest, but will require you to have a high martial spouse set to Chivalry.

description_en: Have max Cohesion in your Bloc
requirements_en: House Bloc has 100.0 Cohesion
how_to_en: Blocs are only available in Japan. Getting to 75 Cohesion is relatively easy, but after that point it is extremely hard to gain Cohesion passively as the game will more or less manipulate the numbers so that it evens out to zero. Instead, You need to use the Bloc Expansion Casus Belli, which is available to Soryo governors. as victory with this CB gives a special event and +5.0 Cohesion instantly. To get the achievement, start as any ruler of sizable strength (Northern Japan's Duchy-tier rulers in 1066 or 1178 are good choices), wait until your Bloc Cohesion is at 75 through passive means, then simply declare and win several Bloc Expansion wars until Cohesion reaches 100. Ensure that you only declare war on realms that are not part of a Bloc, otherwise you will probably lose.

Try to do this to a house that isn't in a bloc, as that'd complicate actually winning the war. Once you're at feuding status you can just declare war once you're stronger & take their capital to win. Creating a custom martial/intrigue character should make this whole process quicker. This can be done anywhere, as long as you have a bad enough relationship with a landed ruler. description_en: Found the Dai Viet Empire
requirements_en: Owns the Dai Viet Empire
how_to_en: The only things required for this decision are owning the Kingdoms of Viet and Champa as an independent Viet culture character with a realm capital somewhere in Viet. Start as Viet in either 1066 or 1178, either with the historical rulers or a custom war-focused character, and conquer the rather small kingdom of Champa with county expansion Casus Bellis. 1178 affords the quickest start, especially with a custom character, as Champa doesn't start as your Tributary and you won't have to wait for a truce before beginning your conquest. It will nonetheless probably take longer to get the 2000 Prestige required for the decision than it will to meet the territorial requirements. Holding a Coronation and swearing an Expand the Realm Oath before you start the first war is one of the most useful steps towards collecting Prestige and ensuring Realm stability.

description_en: Become Shōgun, holding the Empire of Japan as Minamoto no Yoritomo in 1178
requirements_en: Playing as Minamoto no Yoritomo Took the Formalize Shogunate decision
how_to_en: Upon game start, immediately leave your current Bloc and found a new one by inviting independent (i.e, not part of a Bloc) fellow vassals to it. Start hunts and other prestige-gaining activities, and take the Assert Regional Dominion decision when available. Additionally, ensure that all of the House Heads in your Bloc follow the same Aspiration as you, accomplished via the "Demand Aspiration Change" interaction. This will cause your Bloc Cohesion to rapidly rise, and a Cohesion of 75 is required for the next step of the achievement. Once your Bloc has greater military strength than the current Daijo-daijin, start an "Install Ruling Regent" faction and press your demands. Win the war if necessary, and relish in your new title as Daijo-daijin. You now have to meet a few conditions, including having both Legitimacy and Prestige at level 4, and having the third level of crown authority (Powerful Ministries) - Japan starts at level 2. These are relatively easy to accomplish with activities, and once met, you can take the Formalize Shogunate decision. Must be accomplished within the lifetime of Minamoto no Yorimoto.

description_en: Reach the highest level of Merit
requirements_en: Merit Rank 1
how_to_en: You need to play as a vassal with Celestial government. Pick or create a character with high Learning. Start by completing the available Examinations and using the Study scheme. Join every Examination you see, prioritizing Capital Examinations if one is happening, and always pick options that give Merit. Once appointed to a governorship, either recruit Men-at-Arms (if a Military governor) or build buildings and increase your tax income (if any other type of governor) to rapidly increase your passive Merit gain. More expensive Men-at-Arms have a greater impact on monthly Merit gain, so you should buy the most expensive Men-at-Arms you can. Black Armoured Cavalry are a good choice, as they are the most expensive. Continue this cycle until you reach the highest rank. It will take quite a while (50+ years), so be patient. Hosting Examinations and Inspections are additional ways of getting Merit.

description_en: As a Mandala ruler, have a Godlike level of piety, rule an empire, and have 100 radiance
requirements_en: Mandala government Godlike level of devotion 100 Mandala Radiance Owns an empire
how_to_en: A ruler must die with the Religious Icon level of devotion to unlock Divine. Their heir must die with Divine to unlock Demigod. And their heir must die with Demigod to unlock Godlike. They must also die in a way that is acceptable by their Mandala Aspect, however Natural Causes (essentially anything that doesn't result from a war, scheme, activity, or disease) is acceptable to all Aspects. Piety is gained through Mandala Rituals, Tributaries, and Pilgrimages. This is a major bug in the game not upgrading the max piety level even when it should have. A current workaround is to use the designated successor button to pick an heir that the game does recognize. This have to be done for every succession right now, due to the bug.
