description_en: Starting as the Chrysanthemum Throne, restore direct imperial rule
requirements_en: Owns the Empire of Japan title Taken the Restore Direct Imperial Rule decision
starting_conditions_en: The Chrysanthemum Throne
how_to_en: There are two ways to go about this, which differ based on the starting date, your patience, and whether you'd like to enjoy some historical flavor. Do not start in 1178, as the realm will already be Feudal (Soryo) and the achievement will be quite difficult. Starting in 867 as Korehito, use political schemes and influence to get yourself to the front of the line of succession as soon as possible. The reigning Kampaku is old and will die within a few years of game start. Once elected to the regency, choose to abdicate, passing the ceremonial throne to your heir whilst keeping control over the Empire itself (this is a historical method of imperial rule that Japan actually used for a while). You now want to ensure your heir is first in line for the Empire, whilst biding your time as you await the Early Medieval era and the Royal Prerogative innovation. Once you have this innovation and have subsequently died and started playing as your heir (who must now hold both the Empire and the Chrysanthemum Throne--DO NOT abdicate), you will be able to pass the third level of crown authority and take the decision. Starting as Chikahito in 1066, you can either use politics to be elected to the throne as above (difficult as there are less Japanese rulers in 1066 with access to influence to help you), or use the Depose scheme (Intrigue lifestyle focus will help with this) to overthrow the ruling Kampaku. Either way, once on the throne you should already have access to the decision, if not, use activities to boost prestige to enact the requisite laws and take the decision. If starting in this date, do not abdicate upon ascending the throne - you already have Royal Prerogative unlocked and there is no need to abdicate to ensure your line can stay on the throne.

description_en: As Jarl Hæsteinn, become Hegemon of China
requirements_en: Owns the Hegemony of China title
starting_conditions_en: Playing as Jarl Hæsteinn of Montaigu in 867
how_to_en: The main part of this achievement is to get to China in time before you die or it explodes. You can use the Adventure CB more than once, to make sure you're out of range for the King of France which will try his best to kill you. You can use the regular "take county" CB to puncture your way, hopping from county to county (e.g. Dalmatia -> Sinai -> Oman -> Gujarat -> Bengal -> Vietnam). Try to not use your special units as they don't repopulate. Once you border China you will get the Claim the Mandate CB. Use it, use your armies strategically to defeat an enemy, then take some land and you can enforce demands, getting the achievement Note that despite the achievement being worded as "Starting as Hæsteinn" it is actually "Playing as Hæsteinn".

description_en: Claim the Mandate of Heaven as the Head of Faith of a Christian faith
requirements_en: Owns the Hegemony of China title
how_to_en: Can be combined with Fishing in China achievement. Set the Rule Conquerors: Frequency to None or Vicayâla Chola will be a Conqueror and possibly block you from taking the optimal Holy Site. Playing as Hæsteinn, make your way towards India. Take a break after an Adventure CB on a Tribal Duchy on the eastern African coast which will allow you to Raid with the Capture Intent. Make as many prisoners as possible, execute them and undertake Pilgrimages until you have 4000 Piety. Continue and conquer the Holy site of Kerala of Nestorianism in the Raj of Chera Nadu. Then continue without any further Adventure CBs towards the border of China. Once you have the Claim the Mandate CB, convert to Nestorianism, then create a new Christian Faith with you as temporal Head of Faith (new Faiths can't be created while at war). Reset your perks to take the Apostate and Prophet Perks (Hæsteinn already has 7 Learning Perks because the game wants him to have Whole of Body) to reduce costs.
